_comment: Pre-biopsy diagnostic profile: 68M heavy smoker with 3 episodes of gross painless hematuria + cystoscopy showing 2.8 cm bladder mass on right wall. Engine should match WORKUP-SUSPECTED-UROTHELIAL and emit Diagnostic Brief — TURBT (transurethral resection) for histology + muscle invasion assessment + FGFR3/PDL1/HER2 panel before staging Tx (NMIBC intravesical BCG vs MIBC neoadjuvant cisplatin chemo + cystectomy vs metastatic systemic).

suspicion.presentation: 68M heavy smoker (45 pack-years, current) presents to urology with 3 episodes of gross_painless_hematuria over 6 weeks (visible blood in urine, no clots, no dysuria, no flank pain). PCP UA showed 50+ RBC/hpf, no infection. Urine cytology atypical urothelial cells, suspicious for malignancy. CT urogram: 2.8 cm right_lateral_wall_bladder_mass with no obvious wall invasion on CT (need TURBT for muscle invasion assessment). No upper tract lesions. No regional LAD. Cystoscopy in clinic confirmed papillary mass with sessile component. Patient counseled for TURBT + bimanual EUA to assess for cT2+ disease. No prior bladder cancer, no occupational chemical exposure.
